sessions.lever: explored 4 reorderings to break the v0/v1 rotation; none beat floor 9

reviewer.evidence: No candidate adopted (no floor improvement); nothing to review. HEAD pins are the documented cheat; no new cheat introduced.

remaining_gap.summary: Register-rotation + scheduling: target puts the arg0[] loads in v0 and mask(0x10FFFF) in v1 with `lui v1,0x10` interleaved between loads; unpinned GCC swaps (loads->v1, mask->v0) and emits the mask as a separate non-interleaved block. Masking normalizes register names but not the interleaving, so the masked diff is a genuine 9.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: Two register pins (t->$2, mask->$3) are load-bearing; no regfix rules. Pure-C reordering does not reproduce the rotation.

next_hypotheses: Directed permuter from a clean single-function target (text1b.c is high-cascade; register-rotation campaigns elsewhere plateaued large).
-da RTL dump to expose the v0/v1 cycle and the force behind the swap.

Family-level canonical/plateau decision: the whole sibling set (func_80061658/710/617C8/618B4/611A4/6133C) shares the t=$2 / mask=$3 pin pattern; func_800618B4 already parked as a register-rotation pin cluster.

rejected_forms.form: split t into t0/t1/t2 single-def temps
compute mask= early (after call, before loads)
inline loads directly into global stores (no t temp)
